page: Amazon Web Services m7a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor architecture. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and memory subsystem capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU scoring 3077.37 and Geekbench single-core reaching 1936.0, both placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing also show strong results. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, fall into the average tier. A key hardware tradeoff is the memory bandwidth performance; while cached small-block reads are average, uncached large-block read and write bandwidths are weak, measuring 32.75 GB/sec and 25.32 GB/sec respectively.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m7a.xlarge offers dedicated compute resources and modern DDR5 memory without the premium associated with specialized storage or accelerator hardware. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and basic database operations that benefit from high single-core execution speeds but do not demand high memory bandwidth or local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core scores in the top 25%. Conversely, uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths are weak, representing a key hardware tradeoff. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. It delivers average multi-core performance and database operations, making it a balanced option for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources without high memory bandwidth demands.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance, though it exhibits weak uncached memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A dedicated AMD-powered instance offering strong single-core performance and balanced DDR5 memory for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Four dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 cores running at 3.7 GHz
Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Weak uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidth
No local storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Appropriate for web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose applications
